metadata.title:
OSTRANDER HAS


AMENDMENTS

WOULD APPLY CIVIL SERVICE
8 RULE AND LIMIT NUMBER OF


SALOONS BY SPECIAL CHAR.


TER PROVISION.

pages.raw_text:
Councilman Ostrander has under
contemplation the introduction OF
two important amendments to the
city charter.


One is the application, of civil
service rules to all city employees
and the other is the limitation of
the number of saloons.


In the first instance he believes
civil service qualification for the
various branches of public work
would result in marked improve
ment, but it not SO certain about
applying it to appointive heads of
departments. However, he can't
see why qualifying treasurer or
comptroller should not work as
well as qualifying the man who
works with a shovel.


The proposed saloon amendment
is designed to take the place of
the Lambert ordinance that ap-
pears to have persisted by the way
side. Mr.. Ostrander would limit
saloons to the present number for
a certain term Of years and then
allow but one additional saloon per
1000 inhabitants. He believes that
in this manner the increase of sa-
looms beyond the actual demand
could be restricted, and a value
added to the licenses authorized
that would justify the city in de-
manding higher license fee. 1t is
the councilman's intention to have
his amendments in shape to bring
before the people at the coming
spring election.
